title: Tofu Scramble with Veggies (Vegan High-Protein Breakfast) | Eati
introduction: A tofu scramble with veggies is a 260-calorie, 22g-protein plant-based breakfast that cooks in 10 minutes. Firm tofu crumbles into egg-like curds, turmeric gives it the golden color, and bell peppers, onion, and spinach add volume and nutrition. It's the go-to vegan breakfast for anyone wanting high protein without dairy or eggs.

sections.heading: Ingredients (1 serving)
sections.content:
- 7 oz (200g) firm or extra-firm tofu, drained and pressed
- 1/2 bell pepper, diced
- 1/4 medium yellow onion, diced
- 1 cup (30g) fresh spinach
- 1 tsp olive oil
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1/2 tsp garlic powder
- 1/4 tsp onion powder
- 1 tbsp nutritional yeast
- 1 tbsp unsweetened almond milk (for creaminess)
- Salt and black pepper
- Optional: hot sauce, fresh chives, 1/4 avocado sliced

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Press tofu for 10 min (wrap in paper towels + place weight on top). Drain liquid.
2. Heat olive oil in a nonstick pan over medium heat.
3. Sauté onion 2 minutes until translucent. Add bell pepper, cook 2 more minutes.
4. Crumble tofu into the pan with your hands (aim for egg-sized pieces).
5. Add turmeric, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Stir to coat evenly.
6. Cook 4–5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tofu is lightly golden.
7. Add spinach + nutritional yeast + almond milk. Stir until spinach wilts (1 min).
8. Top with hot sauce or avocado. Log in Eati.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 260 kcal per serving (base recipe)
Fat: 12g
Protein: 22g
Carbs: 14g (5g fiber)

With 1/4 avocado: 340 cal, 22g protein. Without oil (water sauté): 215 cal, 22g protein.

sections.heading: Why Tofu Scramble Is the Best Plant-Based Breakfast
sections.content:
Plant-based breakfasts often fall short on protein — most vegan options hover at 8–15g. Tofu scramble delivers 22g, closing the gap with egg-based options while staying fully plant-based.

Macro breakdown:
• 260 calories
• 22g protein (complete)
• 12g fat (mostly plant-based unsaturated)
• 14g carbs, 5g fiber
• ~8.5g protein per 100 calories

Tofu's nutritional profile:
• 7 oz firm tofu: 160 cal, 18g protein, 9g fat.
• Complete protein: all 9 essential amino acids.
• Low in saturated fat.
• No cholesterol.
• Calcium-fortified versions: 35% DV calcium per serving.
• Isoflavones: polyphenols linked to lower heart disease and certain cancers.

Why tofu scramble tastes egg-like:
• Turmeric provides the yellow color.
• Kala namak (black salt) gives a sulfur 'egg' flavor if you want that taste.
• Nutritional yeast adds cheesy/umami notes.
• Firm tofu's crumbly texture mimics scrambled eggs surprisingly well.

Compared to common vegan breakfasts:
• Oatmeal with nut butter: 400 cal, 12g protein.
• Vegan yogurt bowl: 300 cal, 6g protein.
• Smoothie bowl: 450 cal, 12g protein.
• This recipe: 260 cal, 22g protein.

Tofu vs eggs (head to head):
• 3 whole eggs (scramble): 220 cal, 18g protein, 15g fat.
• 7 oz firm tofu (scramble): 160 cal, 18g protein, 9g fat.
• Tofu wins on calories and saturated fat; eggs win on vitamin D, choline, and B12.

For non-vegan alternatives, see [scrambled eggs with mushrooms](/blog/scrambled-eggs-mushrooms-recipe) and [20 high-protein low-calorie breakfast ideas](/blog/20-high-protein-low-calorie-breakfast-ideas).

sections.heading: Choosing and Pressing Tofu Correctly
sections.content:
Tofu has texture levels just like steak has doneness. For scrambles, pick the right type:

Tofu firmness guide:

1. Silken tofu:
• Custard-like texture.
• Falls apart if scrambled.
• Best for smoothies, dips, and desserts.
• Not for scrambling.

2. Soft tofu:
• Like flan.
• Too wet for scrambles.
• Good for miso soup.

3. Medium tofu:
• Beginner-friendly for scrambles.
• Smaller curds, softer texture.

4. Firm tofu (IDEAL for scrambles):
• Holds together when crumbled.
• Absorbs flavor well.
• Most recipes call for this.

5. Extra-firm tofu:
• Denser, chewier curds.
• Most egg-like texture.
• Requires less pressing.

6. Super-firm / high-protein tofu (Nasoya, Wildwood):
• Pre-pressed, 17g protein per 3 oz.
• Ready to crumble.
• Best choice if available.

Pressing technique (critical):
Tofu water-logs during packaging. Remove it for proper texture:

Quick method (10 min):
• Wrap block in paper towels or clean cloth.
• Place between 2 plates with a heavy book on top.
• Wait 10–15 minutes.
• Remove and crumble.

Oven method (20 min):
• Crumble tofu directly onto baking sheet.
• Bake at 400°F for 15 min until mostly dry.
• Add to pan with spices.

No-press hack:
• Buy extra-firm or super-firm tofu.
• Gently squeeze out excess liquid in your hands.
• Crumble directly into hot oil — the initial sizzle evaporates residual water.

What to look for:
• Organic, non-GMO is ideal.
• Water-packed tofu stays freshest.
• Check expiration dates — fresh tofu has better texture.
• Opened tofu: store submerged in water, change daily, use within 5 days.

Sprouted vs regular:
• Sprouted tofu (House Foods Sprouted): easier to digest, slightly more protein.
• Regular tofu: cheaper, widely available, equally good for scrambles.

sections.heading: 7 Tofu Scramble Variations
sections.content:
Build endless versions on this base:

1. Classic Southwest (this recipe, 260 cal, 22g protein):
• Base + bell pepper + onion + spinach + turmeric.

2. Mexican-style (285 cal, 23g protein):
• Base + diced jalapeño + black beans (1/4 cup) + salsa + cilantro + cumin.

3. Mediterranean (290 cal, 24g protein):
• Base + cherry tomatoes + kalamata olives + vegan feta + oregano.

4. Mushroom-spinach (280 cal, 23g protein):
• Base + 1 cup sliced mushrooms + extra spinach + fresh thyme.

5. Breakfast burrito (400 cal, 28g protein):
• Base + rolled in low-carb tortilla + avocado + salsa. Inspired by [turkey & egg breakfast wrap](/blog/turkey-egg-breakfast-wrap-recipe).

6. Indian-inspired (290 cal, 23g protein):
• Base + 1/2 tsp garam masala + 1/4 tsp cumin + peas + diced tomato.

7. Kimchi scramble (275 cal, 23g protein):
• Base + 1/4 cup chopped kimchi + scallions + sesame oil finish.

Plate variations (choose 1 side):
• +130 cal: 1 slice whole-grain toast with 1 tsp vegan butter.
• +200 cal: 1/2 cup diced sweet potato (roasted).
• +100 cal: 1/2 cup cooked quinoa.
• +60 cal: 1 cup mixed berries on the side.
• +120 cal: 1/4 avocado sliced.

Protein-boosting add-ins:
• +9g protein: 2 tbsp hemp hearts (+90 cal).
• +5g protein: 2 tbsp nutritional yeast (+40 cal).
• +15g protein: 1 scoop unflavored plant protein powder stirred in (+110 cal).
• +7g protein: 1/4 cup cooked edamame (+50 cal).

Pro flavor tip:
Add 1/2 tsp kala namak (black salt) at the end for an authentic 'egg' sulfur flavor. Find it at Indian grocery stores or Amazon for ~$5 a jar. A jar lasts 6 months and transforms tofu scrambles.

sections.heading: Meal Prep and Storage Tips
sections.content:
Tofu scrambles reheat better than regular egg scrambles — making them excellent for meal prep:

Sunday batch (4 servings):
1. Double the recipe (use 14 oz tofu).
2. Cook as directed.
3. Cool completely before portioning.
4. Divide into 4 glass containers.
5. Store up to 4 days in fridge.

Reheating options:
• Microwave: 90 seconds covered.
• Stovetop: 3 min in nonstick pan with splash of water.
• Both retain texture well.

Freezing:
• Yes, tofu scramble freezes for 2 months.
• Texture changes slightly (denser) but still good.
• Thaw overnight in fridge before reheating.

Veggie shortcuts:
• Pre-diced mirepoix (onion + celery + carrot): sold in grocery stores.
• Frozen diced bell peppers: same nutrition, no chopping.
• Pre-washed baby spinach: grab-and-add.
• Frozen minced garlic cubes: 1 cube = 1 clove.

Smart shopping:
• Firm tofu blocks: $2.50 each = $0.60/serving (cheapest protein in the scramble).
• Organic non-GMO: slight premium but worth it for daily eaters.
• Nutritional yeast: 1 container ($6) lasts 2 months for daily use.
• Turmeric: $5 jar lasts 6 months.

Total cost per serving:
• Tofu: $0.60.
• Veggies: $0.80.
• Spices + nutritional yeast: $0.20.
• Total: ~$1.60/serving.

Compared to:
• Vegan breakfast sandwich at coffee shop: $6–8.
• Impossible Foods breakfast patty: $4 each.

Travel-friendly:
• Pre-cooked scramble in microwave-safe container: reheats anywhere.
• Pair with a slice of toast at destination for a complete meal.

For more plant-forward breakfast ideas, see [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods) and [best foods for weight loss](/blog/best-foods-for-weight-loss).

midArticleCta: Plant-based and 22g of protein — track this tofu scramble in Eati to see how it stacks up in your daily plan.
conclusion: A 10-minute, 260-calorie vegan breakfast with 22g of protein rivals any egg-based option for satiety and nutrition. Keep a block of firm tofu and some pre-diced veggies on hand, and you have the best plant-based breakfast ready in under 15 minutes.
metaTitle: Tofu Scramble with Veggies: 22g Protein, 260 Calories (Vegan Breakfast)
metaDescription: High-protein vegan tofu scramble with veggies — 22g protein in 260 calories. Includes tofu-buying guide, pressing technique, 7 variations, and meal prep tips.

faqs.question: How many calories in a tofu scramble with veggies?
faqs.answer: About 260 calories per serving with 7 oz firm tofu, bell pepper, onion, spinach, and spices. Delivers 22g of complete plant-based protein. Adding 1/4 avocado brings it to 340 cal with 22g protein.

faqs.question: How do you make tofu scramble taste like eggs?
faqs.answer: Three key ingredients: (1) 1/2 tsp turmeric for the yellow color, (2) 1/2 tsp kala namak (black salt) for sulfur 'egg' flavor, (3) 1 tbsp nutritional yeast for umami richness. Cook firm tofu in egg-sized crumbles for the best texture.

faqs.question: Is tofu scramble good for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Yes. 22g protein in 260 calories is excellent, and tofu is lower in saturated fat and calories than eggs. It's especially useful for plant-based eaters who struggle to hit protein targets at breakfast. The fiber from veggies adds satiety.

faqs.question: Do I have to press tofu for scramble?
faqs.answer: Yes, or use pre-pressed/super-firm tofu. Pressing removes excess water so the tofu browns instead of steaming. Quick method: wrap in paper towels, place between plates with a heavy book on top, wait 10–15 minutes.

faqs.question: Can I meal prep tofu scramble?
faqs.answer: Yes — tofu scramble reheats better than egg scramble. Cook a 4-serving batch, divide into glass containers, and refrigerate up to 4 days. Reheat 90 seconds in the microwave. Freezes for 2 months (texture gets slightly denser but still good).
